title: PM Lane 180 - Local Field Prep Queue Grouping
authority: repo technical authority; PM lane implementation authority; final integration authority
objective: Reduce Project Miner Temp Power field-prep queue scan burden by grouping the existing Local Field Prep Queue without changing derived status logic, queue counts, export references, disclosure behavior, anchor behavior, storage posture, or field-authorization/write boundaries.
scope: Grouped the existing Local Field Prep Queue into Field Prep Inputs, Kickoff Artifact, and Authority And Production Boundary.

Preserved the same five derived queue cards, status logic, summary count, #field-prep anchor, disclosure behavior, no-storage posture, export references, read seams, and no-field-authorization/no-write language.
Kept the Local Field Prep Queue disclosure behavior stateless with no field-prep-queue disclosure localStorage key.
Used a read-only sidecar to verify the grouping shape and next safe lane while VS Code Codex retained PM implementation and final integration authority.
guardrails: No Supabase, Render, Vercel, or Olares product action.
No approval POST or approval-row creation.

No field authorization, assignment, schedule/status, durable field record, production tracking, customer report, completion evidence, billing, payroll, invoice, accounting, or external finance-system write.
No new export action, export handler, filename, payload version, localStorage key, backend route, service/auth/ingress change, or autonomous AI business-state mutation.
